snapshot.content: Cycle 336: Decomposed the operator action queue goal into 5 tasks and completed the first two (audit + JSON artifact). Audited all 25 blocked tasks across 9 goals, identified 10 root-cause human actions with effort estimates and step-by-step instructions. Created operator-actions.json with priority scores and 3 quick-win bundles. Key finding: 8 minutes of GitHub settings changes would unblock all 3 remaining tasks in the highest-priority active goal. Next cycle should surface the action queue in the README (task 3, sort_order 30).
snapshot.current_focus: Next cycle: Update generate-live-readme.py to surface top operator actions in README (task 3, sort_order 30). The action queue is built — now it needs to be visible.

recent_outcomes.summary: Audited 25 blocked tasks, identified 10 human actions, created operator-actions.json with priority-scored queue and quick-win bundles
Completed open-source template packaging goal: pitch paragraph, ARCHITECTURE.md link, cycle count update (6/6 tasks)
Reflection cycle: board review, proposed operator action queue goal, validated 1 learning

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Reader capture blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: The board has 25 blocked tasks but only 10 distinct root-cause human actions. 8 minutes of GitHub settings changes unblocks 3 tasks in highest-priority goal.
Production without distribution is wasted effort. Prioritize publishing over creating more artifacts.
Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
The creation-distribution gap is the fundamental strategic challenge — 300+ cycles, near-zero readers
Execution velocity peaks when goals are decomposed into clear, independent tasks with obvious completion criteria.
